zh.Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Engla: 英國格盧斯特斯爾可爾 聖 阿爾德恩斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Lugg於1440年出生在英國賀福爾盧沃迪時，他的父親Thomas Lugg16歲，母親Loretta Rose Lehmann15歲。他於1462年與Alexandria Alice Vaughan在英國格盧斯特斯爾可爾 聖 阿爾德恩斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Engla: 英国格盧斯特斯爾可爾 聖 阿爾德恩斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Lugg于1440年出生在英国賀福爾盧沃迪时，他的父亲Thomas Lugg16岁，母亲Loretta Rose Lehmann15岁。他于1462年与Alexandria Alice Vaughan在英国格盧斯特斯爾可爾 聖 阿爾德恩斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Lugg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): probably from a local vernacular derivative of Lucas . However, Reaney posits an Old English personal name,Lugga, from which this name could be derived.

variants.name: Lucas
meanings: English, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Lucas (Greek Loukas) ‘man from Lucania’. Lucania is a region of southern Italy thought to have been named in ancient times with a word meaning ‘bright’ or ‘shining’ (compare Lucio ). The Christian name owed its enormous popularity throughout Europe in the Middle Ages to Saint Luke the Evangelist, hence the development of this surname and many vernacular derivatives in most of the languages of Europe. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Loukas , Hungarian Lukács (see Lukacs ), German, Dutch, etc. Lukas , Polish Łukasz, Czech and Slovak Lukáš, Czech Lukeš, Slovenian and Croatian Lukež (see Lukes ). Compare Luke .
Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lùcais (see McLucas ).
History: Jacques Lucas dit Lépine from Port-en-Bessin-Huppain in Calvados, France, married Françoise Capel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1653.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Lugg was born in 1440, in Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England, his father, Thomas Lugg, was 16 and his mother, Loretta Rose Lehmann, was 15. He married Alexandria Alice Vaughan in 1462, in Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Lugg est né en 1440, à Lugwardine, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Lugg, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Loretta Rose Lehmann, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Alexandria Alice Vaughan en 1462, à Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Lugg 1440 in Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Lugg 16 und seine Mutter Loretta Rose Lehmann 15. Er hat 1462 in Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, England Alexandria Alice Vaughan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Lugg è nato il nel 1440 nel Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Lugg, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Loretta Rose Lehmann, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Alexandria Alice Vaughan nel 1462, nel Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ja.Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England: イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアルグワーディン
ja.Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Engla: イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアコルン スト アルドウイン'ス
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Luggが1440年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアルグワーディンで生まれた時，父Thomas Luggは16歳，母Loretta Rose Lehmannは15歳であった。1462年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアコルン スト アルドウイン'スでAlexandria Alice Vaughanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Lugg(이)가 1440년 영국 허퍼드실르 루그워딘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Lugg(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Loretta Rose Lehmann(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1462년 영국 그루스스터실르 코컨 스트 알드w이'스에서 Alexandria Alice Vaughan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Lugg nasceu em 1440, em Lugwardine, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Lugg, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Loretta Rose Lehmann, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Alexandria Alice Vaughan em 1462, em Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Lugg родился в 1440 г. в местности лугвардаин, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Lugg было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Loretta Rose Lehmann, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Alexandria Alice Vaughan в 1462 г. в местности колн ст алдуин'с, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Lugg nació en 1440, en Lugwardine, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Lugg, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Loretta Rose Lehmann, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Alexandria Alice Vaughan en 1462, en Coln St Aldwyn's, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
